Comment: This submission includes internal variability in surface temperature enabled.

Name: Chris Smith
Comment: In the time axis, concentrations, forcing, temperature, heat content and energy variables are given on "timebounds", which are the edge of year boundaries. Emissions are given on "timepoints", which are half-years. Where any greenhouse gas is specified in a scenario using a concentration-driven pathway, equivalent emissions are automatically calculated and reported on timepoints. Therefore such scenarios contain both full and half-years in the time axis, and have alternating NaN values for the time variable not reported. The time axis metadata (days or hours since 1750-01-01) is correct and should be consulted in this case. Interpolating values to fill in the NaNs should be acceptable though will likely smooth out the internal variability in temperature. If you require mid-year values for temperature, energy, concentration, forcing etc., do the interpolation and report on the half-year/timepoint.
Scenario: all runs that include concentration-driven greenhouse gas pathways (idealised or scenario)
